data.title: White House to hold meeting with banks and crypto companies regarding CLARITY Act issues. Lawmakers divided on stablecoin interest regulations.
data.description: Efforts are underway by the Trump administration to resolve a stalemate regarding legislation on the structure of the cryptocurrency market. Lawmakers are currently divided on regulations concerning interest rates for stablecoins, with significant disagreement on the issue.

data.title: Mitsubishi Electric acquire Nozomi Networks, which surpassed $100M revenue, to deliver OT/IoT cybersecurity globally.
data.description: Nozomi Networks has reached a significant milestone by exceeding $100 million in revenue. Operating as a standalone entity, this subsidiary will focus on providing cybersecurity solutions for operational technology and Internet of Things systems to its diverse customer and partner base worldwide. The company remains committed to delivering vendor-neutral services and products. This development was announced in San Francisco on January 29, 2026.

data.title: HYPE token surges 60% from staking and balance-sheet buying. Sustainability questioned with flat open interest.
data.description: The HYPE token from Hyperliquid saw a significant increase of 60% in value. This surge was attributed to a high level of staking and the accumulation of assets. Despite this rise, open interest and perpetual volumes did not see much change and remained stable.

data.title: Alm. Brand A/S reports positive Q4 2025 results in Personal and Commercial Lines, achieving strategic goals.
data.description: Consistent growth in core operations within Personal Lines and Commercial Lines results in a robust conclusion to the strategic period.

data.title: Robinhood's CEO calls GameStop incident a wake-up call for tokenization. Experts cite undercapitalization issue.
data.description: Robinhood has attributed the GameStop crisis to infrastructure issues, however, experts argue that undercapitalization is a more significant factor. Robinhood faced challenges in managing the surge in trading activity, with a high number of users involved. The platform's infrastructure struggled to cope with the unprecedented increase, causing limitations on trading and leading to backlash from users. Despite Robinhood's claims, experts believe that the lack of adequate capitalization played a crucial role in the crisis, highlighting the importance of financial stability in trading platforms.

data.title: Aedifica announces Prospectus for Exchange Offer on Cofinimmo shares approved, Initial Acceptance Period starts on 30 Jan. 2026.
data.description: Aedifica's Prospectus for the Exchange Offer regarding Cofinimmo has received approval and is now accessible on the internet. The commencement of the Initial Acceptance Period is scheduled for January 30, 2026.

data.title: OSE Immunotherapeutics plans to focus on Chronic Pouchitis and Hidradenitis Suppurativa for Lusvertikimab treatment.
data.description: OSE Immunotherapeutics has announced the selection of Chronic Pouchitis and Hidradenitis Suppurativa as new key indications for their drug Lusvertikimab. This decision comes after conducting extensive research and assessing the potential of the drug in treating these conditions. Chronic Pouchitis affects approximately 30,000 to 100,000 people in the United States, while Hidradenitis Suppurativa impacts about 200,000 individuals in the country. This strategic move reflects OSE Immunotherapeutics' commitment to addressing significant unmet medical needs and expanding the utility of Lusvertikimab in improving patient outcomes.

data.title: Sanofi reports Q4 sales growth of 13.3%, reaching €1.1 billion with an EPS of €1.53. Vaccines sales decrease by 2.5% with €2.0 billion in sales. Pharma launches increase sales by 49.4% to €1.1 billion. Research and Development expenses reach €2.3 billion, up 6.6%. Business EPS up by 26.7% delivering profitable growth. Sanofi eyes high single-digit sales growth in 2026. Capital allocation includes Dynavax acquisition and €5 billion share buyback program. Proposed dividend of €4.12, up by 5.1%.
data.description: In January 2026, Sanofi reported strong sales growth with a 13.3% increase in Q4 sales at CER and business EPS of €1.53. Pharma launches saw a significant increase in sales by 49.4%, reaching €1.1 billion, mainly due to Ayvakit and ALTUVIIIO. Dupixent sales also increased by 32.2% to €4.2 billion. However, vaccines sales decreased by 2.5% to €2.0 billion, with better performance in influenza than expected. Research and Development expenses increased to €2.3 billion, while selling, general, and administrative expenses reached €2.7 billion. The business EPS was up by 26.7% at CER. Sanofi achieved ten regulatory approvals and positive phase 3 results for various programs. They announced the Dynavax acquisition and completed the Vicebio acquisition, along with a €5 billion share buyback program. They proposed a dividend of €4.12, up by 5.1%. Sanofi expects sales to grow by a high single-digit percentage in 2026 with business EPS growing slightly faster. They plan a €1 billion share buyback program in 2026. CEO Paul Hudson highlighted the strong performance in Q4, driven by new medicines and Dupixent.
